title: Roldana reticulata
taxonomy: Roldana reticulata is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Roldana, which is a group of flowering plants native to Mexico and Central America. Roldana reticulata is further classified as a species within the genus, specifically within the section and series of the plant's classification. The species is native to Mexico and is known for its unique reticulated leaf patterns. The classification of Roldana reticulata is primarily based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf morphology and flowering habits.

intro: Roldana reticulata, also known as Mexican mint or Mexican golden daisy, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The health benefits of Roldana reticulata include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Roldana reticulata is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
